text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Lu-177 PSMA-617 Cycle 2 Post-Treatment Assessment

ATTENDING: [Attending Oncologist]
DIVISION: Genitourinary Medical Oncology

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Mr. Robert Delacroix is a 72-year-old French-Canadian male with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) presenting for routine follow-up approximately 3 weeks after his second cycle of lutetium-177 PSMA-617 (Pluvicto). He reports feeling "a little less exhausted" compared to prior visits, though fatigue remains a significant issue. He also notes persistent dry mouth.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Mr. Delacroix was initially diagnosed with high-grade, metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (Gleason 9, cT3bN1M1b) approximately four years ago. His disease course has been extensive: he received ADT plus abiraterone/prednisone (Zytiga) as frontline therapy with an excellent PSA response (nadir 0.8 ng/mL), followed by progression to castration-resistant disease at roughly 18 months. He was transitioned to enzalutamide (Xtandi), achieving a PSA nadir of 0.6 ng/mL, but developed new bone metastases at T10, T12, and the right 4th rib at approximately 9 months. Genomic testing (germline) confirmed a pathogenic BRCA2 mutation, and he was switched to olaparib (Lynparza) 300 mg BID, later dose-reduced to 200 mg BID due to grade 3 anemia requiring transfusions. He had an early and substantial PSA response to olaparib (nadir ~1.4 ng/mL), but at approximately 8 months he developed visceral progression with new hepatic lesions (segments 5 and 7) and bilateral pulmonary nodules confirmed by CT. Liver biopsy confirmed metastatic prostatic adenocarcinoma (PSA/PSMA/NKX3.1 positive, no neuroendocrine transdifferentiation). Repeat genomic profiling of the liver met showed AR-V7 splice variant positivity and high-level MET amplification as new alterations, explaining resistance to further AR-directed therapy. Olaparib was discontinued. PSMA PET/CT at progression demonstrated widespread PSMA-avid disease including hepatic, pulmonary, nodal, and osseous sites, with high lesion-to-background ratio — patient was felt to be an excellent candidate for radioligand therapy.

He initiated Lu-177 PSMA-617 (Pluvicto) 7.4 GBq IV q6 weeks. He received Cycle 1 without significant acute toxicity. He received Cycle 2 approximately 3 weeks ago. He continues on continuous ADT (Lupron depot 22.5 mg IM q3 months, most recent injection 6 weeks ago) and quarterly zoledronic acid (Zometa). Palliative care has been co-managing his symptom burden. A goals-of-care conversation was initiated at last visit; he and his wife understand the disease is incurable and the current treatment focus is on maintaining quality of life and time at home.

Today he reports:
- Fatigue: grade 2, present most days, somewhat improved from the period just prior to starting Pluvicto; he is able to manage light household activity
- Dry mouth (xerostomia): grade 1, onset after Cycle 1, persistent; managed with frequent sips of water and sugar-free lozenges; no significant difficulty swallowing
- Appetite: modestly improved compared to nadir; wife reports he is eating about 75% of usual intake; no nausea/vomiting
- Back pain: improved; residual mild discomfort in thoracic region (grade 1), well-controlled on scheduled acetaminophen 650 mg TID; no worsening of thoracic or radicular symptoms since completion of palliative EBRT to T10-T12 several months ago
- No hematuria, dysuria, urinary retention
- No new neurological symptoms; no bowel/bladder incontinence
- No hemoptysis
- No dyspnea at rest; mild exertional dyspnea with climbing one flight of stairs (likely multifactorial: anemia and deconditioning)
- Bowel function: mildly constipated, managed with MiraLax
- Hot flashes: persistent grade 2-3, chronic, stable
- Mood: patient describes himself as "at peace" with situation; wife present and engaged

No acute radiation toxicity symptoms (skin changes over treated field have resolved). No radiation-induced myelopathy.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Positive as above. Additionally:
- Constitutional: fatigue, mild weight loss (approximately 6 kg over the prior 3 months, now weight appears stable on today's measurement), decreased appetite as noted
- HEENT: xerostomia grade 1, no parotid swelling, no ageusia
- Respiratory: mild exertional dyspnea, no cough, no hemoptysis
- GI: decreased appetite, mild constipation; no nausea, no abdominal pain, no jaundice
- Musculoskeletal: thoracic back pain grade 1, stable; no new bone pain
- Neuro: no new focal deficits, no cognitive complaints beyond background mild sluggishness attributed to chronic ADT
- Hematologic: no bleeding, no bruising; prior transfusion-dependent anemia on olaparib, last transfusion event approximately 3 months prior (at time of olaparib discontinuation)
- GU: no lower urinary tract symptoms beyond mild reduced stream (chronic, unchanged)

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
VS: BP 134/82 mmHg, HR 74 bpm (regular), RR 16, O2 sat 96% on room air, Temp 36.8°C, Weight 83 kg (down approximately 6 kg from 3 months ago, appears stable from last visit)
ECOG PS: 2

General: Alert, pleasant, mildly fatigued-appearing male in no acute distress. Sitting comfortably. Wife at bedside.
HEENT: Mild oral mucosal dryness noted. No parotid gland enlargement or tenderness. No oral ulcers. No scleral icterus.
Neck: No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.
Chest: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles or wheeze. Chest wall mildly tender to deep palpation over right posterior 4th rib region (known met, previously irradiated elsewhere — this is a separate site; no new tenderness).
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. Trace bilateral ankle edema (1+).
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Liver span approximately 13 cm by percussion (borderline enlarged). Spleen not palpable. No ascites.
Genitourinary: External genitalia normal for age. No palpable lymphadenopathy in inguinal region. Digital rectal exam deferred (prior exam confirmed fixed prostate in setting of known primary; no clinical indication today).
Back/Spine: Mild thoracic paraspinal tenderness at T10-T12 level, non-radiating. No lumbar percussion tenderness. No saddle anesthesia elicited on history.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. No calf tenderness. No bony deformity of right femur.
Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Strength 4+/5 proximal lower extremities bilaterally (likely deconditioning/anemia). No upper motor neuron signs. Gait slow but steady with cane (established).
Skin: No radiation dermatitis in treated fields. No jaundice. No suspicious lesions.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS
PSA: 11.2 ng/mL (down from 18.4 ng/mL at Lu-177 initiation — 39% decline after 2 cycles)
Testosterone: 7 ng/dL (confirmed castrate)
CBC:
  - Hemoglobin: 8.6 g/dL (grade 2 anemia; stable from prior, no transfusion required this cycle)
  - WBC: 4.8 x10^9/L
  - ANC: 3.1 x10^9/L
  - Platelets: 141 x10^9/L (mild thrombocytopenia grade 1, new — will monitor)
Comprehensive metabolic panel:
  - Creatinine: 1.2 mg/dL, eGFR: ~58 mL/min/1.73m2 (stable, mildly reduced)
  - ALT: 52 U/L (mild elevation grade 1, up slightly from 48 U/L; likely hepatic metastases)
  - AST: 44 U/L (grade 1)
  - Total bilirubin: 0.9 mg/dL (normal)
  - Alkaline phosphatase: 248 U/L (mildly elevated, up from 210 — mixed bone/liver contribution)
  - Albumin: 3.3 g/dL (mildly low; consistent with nutritional state and disease burden)
  - Sodium/Potassium: within normal limits
LDH: 312 U/L (mildly elevated; consistent with disease burden)

---

IMAGING RESULTS
No new imaging obtained at this visit. Most recent relevant imaging: PSMA PET/CT at time of olaparib progression showed widespread PSMA-avid disease with hepatic, pulmonary, nodal, and osseous involvement. CT restaging is planned after Cycle 4 of Lu-177 PSMA-617.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Mr. Delacroix is a 72-year-old man with extensively pretreated mCRPC — post-abiraterone, post-enzalutamide, post-olaparib, s/p palliative EBRT to thoracic spine — now on his third cycle window of Lu-177 PSMA-617 (Pluvicto). He is demonstrating early biochemical response with a PSA decline from 18.4 to 11.2 ng/mL (39% reduction) after 2 cycles. He is tolerating treatment reasonably well for his disease burden.

1. mCRPC — PROGRESSING THROUGH MULTIPLE LINES, ON LU-177 PSMA-617 (PLUVICTO)
   - PSA response encouraging; 39% decline from pre-treatment baseline. Will continue to follow PSA trajectory; appreciate that PSA kinetics on radioligand therapy can lag or fluctuate.
   - ECOG PS 2, stable. No visceral crisis at this time.
   - CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast planned after Cycle 4 to formally assess hepatic and pulmonary lesion response.
   - Continue Lu-177 PSMA-617 7.4 GBq IV — Cycle 3 scheduled in approximately 3 weeks (per 6-week cycle interval). Amino acid infusion for salivary gland protection to continue with each cycle per protocol.
   - ADT: continue leuprolide (Lupron) 22.5 mg IM q3 months; next injection in approximately 6 weeks.
   - Bone-protective therapy: zoledronic acid (Zometa) 4 mg IV — next dose due; will check creatinine day-of; renal function marginally adequate (eGFR ~58, above 30 threshold — ok to proceed; will ensure adequate hydration).

2. ANEMIA — GRADE 2, STABLE
   - Hgb 8.6 g/dL today. No transfusion required this cycle — improvement over the transfusion-dependent picture on olaparib.
   - Likely multifactorial: prior olaparib-related myelosuppression, disease infiltration of bone marrow (extensive osseous mets), and Lu-177 radiation effects on marrow.
   - Will continue to monitor CBC at each visit and prior to each Lu-177 cycle. Transfusion threshold Hgb <7.5 g/dL or symptomatic.
   - New grade 1 thrombocytopenia (plt 141): likely radioligand therapy effect on bone marrow. No intervention at this time; will track closely. If platelets drop below 75 x10^9/L prior to next cycle, will consider dose delay.
   - Continue iron supplementation. Reinforce nutritional counseling.
   - Erythropoiesis-stimulating agent not indicated (active malignancy with potential for thromboembolic risk; prior decision maintained).

3. XEROSTOMIA — GRADE 1
   - Dry mouth attributed to salivary gland PSMA uptake / off-target radiation from Lu-177. Amino acid infusion during infusion (cytoprotective) being utilized.
   - Counsel patient on oral hygiene, sugar-free lozenges/gum, frequent water intake, biotene products. Dental follow-up recommended.
   - Grade 1 manageable; no dose modification for this toxicity alone.

4. BONE METASTASES / SKELETAL HEALTH
   - Continue Zometa q3 months (dental hygiene counseled; no invasive dental procedures).
   - Thoracic back pain grade 1 — adequately controlled on scheduled acetaminophen 650 mg TID. No opioid escalation needed at this time. If pain worsens, will consider low-dose oral morphine or oxycodone.
   - Prior palliative RT to T10-T12 completed; good local response maintained. Right 4th rib met unchanged symptomatically.
   - No new neurological symptoms suggesting cord compromise at this time — no urgent MRI indicated today.

5. NUTRITIONAL / FUNCTIONAL STATUS
   - Weight appears stable at 83 kg today (down 6 kg from 3 months ago, but no further decline this visit). Encouraging.
   - Albumin 3.3 g/dL — mild hypoalbuminemia. Nutritional consult placed; encourage protein-rich diet and oral supplements (e.g., Ensure/Boost).
   - ECOG 2 — continue to engage PT/OT for gentle exercise and fall prevention (on established program).

6. HYPERTENSION
   - On amlodipine 5 mg daily (initiated for enzalutamide-related HTN). BP 134/82 today — reasonably controlled. Continue current regimen.

7. PALLIATIVE CARE / GOALS OF CARE
   - Palliative care team actively co-managing symptom burden. Recommend continued close collaboration.
   - Goals-of-care conversation initiated last visit — patient and wife aware of incurable disease trajectory. Patient remains focused on quality time at home. Full code status to be formally revisited at next palliative care appointment.
   - Social work referral placed to assist with community support services.
   - No hospice transition at this time — patient remains on active disease-directed therapy with functional status ECOG 2 and early treatment response.

FOLLOW-UP: Return to clinic in approximately 3 weeks for pre-cycle 3 assessment. CBC and PSA to be drawn at that visit. Full labs including LFTs and creatinine for Zometa dosing. CT restaging deferred to post-cycle 4 window.

Time spent: 55 minutes total, >50% in counseling and coordination of care.

Electronically signed by: [Attending GU Oncologist], MD
Co-signed by: [Fellow], MD (GU Oncology)
